reasoning: North Korea has not tested a nuclear weapon since 2017, representing a significant pause compared to their earlier testing cadence (6 tests between 2006 and 2017). Although closer alignment with Russia provides them with diplomatic cover at the UN Security Council, opposition from China likely still acts as a strong deterrent against nuclear testing on the peninsula. Furthermore, Pyongyang has recently prioritized advanced missile testing, satellite launches, and conventional arms production (in part to supply Russia). While US and South Korean intelligence indicate that preparations at the Punggye-ri test site have been complete since 2022, the prolonged delay suggests North Korea does not feel an urgent technical or strategic need to test immediately. Therefore, I estimate a 25% chance that a test will occur before July 1, 2025.
